question_text: Does the repo provide hyperparameter sweeps or scaling recipes for each model size (25M / 85M / 170M / 302M / 567M / 3B / 10B), or must the user hand-tune for each scale?

evidence.snippet: (only pyproject.toml exists — no configs/ directory or per-size hyperparameter YAML/JSON files)
notebooks/scaling_laws.ipynb (analysis notebook, not a sweep/recipe config)
notebooks/optimal_lr.ipynb (LR analysis notebook, not per-size recipes)

quick_check_verdict.notes: "No configs/ directory or per-model-size hyperparameter recipe files exist; scaling/LR material lives only in analysis notebooks, so users must hand-derive hyperparameters for each scale.
